cli.notes: Supports multiple output formats including raw binary (-Fbin) for testing

encoding_behaviors.description: With -no-opt, preserves ADD/AND/OR/SUB #imm,Dn as general-purpose encoding
encoding_behaviors.behavior: When assembling <OP> #imm,Dn with -no-opt, vasm uses the general-purpose instruction encoding with immediate EA mode (#imm = mode 111, reg 100), NOT the dedicated immediate instruction
encoding_behaviors.note: With optimizations enabled, vasm may route to ADDI like GenAm does

encoding_behaviors.description: EXG preserves syntactic operand order in Rx/Ry fields
encoding_behaviors.behavior: First operand → Rx field, second operand → Ry field

tested.notes: 27 known divergences: our assembler routes ADD/AND/OR/SUB/CMP/EOR #imm to ADDI/etc (DevPac-style), vasm -no-opt keeps general encoding. Both valid.
